reviews.review_msg: (I took this class fall 2025) I think people misunderstand her, she was a bit scary to me too, but she honestly just seems to want to be fair (eg. People didn't clean despite reminders and were penalized) even then the class average was pretty high (88% befoee the final). Since her grading rubric was really straightforward and easy to understand.

Very harsh and unsympathetic instructor. Even when reached out with issues, the replies felt cold and invalidated. she decides what she thinks of you, that's the tone for the rest of the course. Asking for help  felt almost impossible. If you need any level of understanding or basic flexibility, this class will be extremely stressful. badly taught.
Hope for another prof, strongly not recommended
Dr. K is friendly and helpful. Show up prepared and you will succeed. Go to office hours!!! Best lab course I have taken.
Solid prof. Had Dr. K for 222 and 309 and would take her again. Listen in lecture and go to her office hours. She expects alot but you can do well if your ready to work hard.
Had Dr. K for 222 and took 309 in person. This is a hard course but she makes it bearable and fun. Would take any course with her.

One of the nicest profs I've had. He's very helpful and approachable. His lectures can be dull, due partly to his monotone voice and partly to the fact that we already know 75% of the lecture material. His weekly quizzes can be were fair and so was his final. Note that his lectures normally take 1 hour, not 2.

Avoid if possible. Notes look like a disorganized patchwork of old powerpoint slides from past semesters. Lectures were extremely dull. Exams were unreasonably difficult. Labs weren't bad due to great lab staff. With any luck I'll be able to avoid him for any future academic work.
for the love of god, wait until kovalyova teaches this course and save yourselves a ton of work and effort.
Very great. I waited a year to take MBB 309 with him. He helps a lot, labs are very easy, and he gives you all the dilutions and calculations you need. easy marking, easy quizes, and great overall.

Dr. Leroux is the most sincere and caring prof Ive had during my time at SFU. He explains all concepts exceptionally well and encourages questions during and after class. If you ever have a chance to take his class, do not hesitate. The grading was clear and there are many opportunities for you to get a decent grade.
Dr. Leroux is very enthusiastic about the subject, and explains concepts very clearly. Lectures are very useful, and he gives good feedback. Don't skip lectures and do put in an effort.

Dr. Leroux is a fantastic professor, who is not only incredibly knowledgeable, but genuinely cares that you succeed in the course. MBB 309 is a lab course, so you need to come prepared, having already read and understood the purpose of each step in the experimental protocol. I learned so much in this course and a lot of that is credit to Dr. Leroux
I wish Dr. Leroux would teach more upper MBB courses. Prepare well for the final quiz, which can be challenging.
